data.title: fix(core): stop-git-dirty hook uses invalid output schema
data.description:
## Bug

`plugins/core/hooks/stop-git-dirty.sh` outputs `hookSpecificOutput` with `hookEventName: "Stop"`, but `Stop` hooks don't support the `hookSpecificOutput` schema.

### Error

```
Stop hook error: JSON validation failed: Hook JSON output validation failed:
  - : Invalid input
```

### Current output (invalid)

```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "Stop",
    "additionalContext": "Note: there are uncommitted changes..."
  }
}
```

### Valid Stop hook schema

Per [Claude Code docs](<https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference>), `hookSpecificOutput` is only valid for:

* `PreToolUse`
* `UserPromptSubmit`
* `PostToolUse`

`Stop` hooks should use top-level fields:

```json
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "reason": "There are uncommitted changes in the working tree:\n...\nRemind the user if relevant."
}
```

### Fix

In `stop-git-dirty.sh`, replace the `jq` output block:

```bash
jq -n --arg dirty "$dirty" '{
  "decision": "approve",
  "reason": "Note: there are uncommitted changes in the working tree:\n\($dirty)\nRemind the user if relevant."
}'
```

Using `"decision": "approve"` keeps it non-blocking (informational only).
